claim_gate.required_statement: State the problem as a uniform single-instance computational decoding conjecture with explicit preprocessing/advice exclusions; supply a natural reduction or named hardness assumption before treating it as more than an oracle separation.

classical_upper_bounds.access_model: classical evaluator oracle returning (x+s)^e
classical_upper_bounds.implication: A uniform deterministic recovery upper bound is already domain-scale for fixed character order.

classical_upper_bounds.access_model: classical shifted-power evaluator oracle
classical_upper_bounds.implication: For e=(p-1)/character_order, classical query complexity is logarithmic even though the known uniform decoding time remains domain-linear.

classical_upper_bounds.access_model: classical evaluator oracle plus proposed candidate t
classical_upper_bounds.implication: Sublinear candidate verification exists, but testing all p candidates is not a poly(log p) decoder.

classical_upper_bounds.access_model: fixed chosen queries with modulus-dependent preprocessing/advice
classical_upper_bounds.implication: Any online hardness claim must exclude exponential advice/preprocessing and state amortization assumptions.
classical_upper_bounds.preprocessing_bound: Theta(p log p) labels plus shift dictionary
classical_upper_bounds.query_bound: empirical O(log p) fixed prefix on tested rows
classical_upper_bounds.time_bound: online O(log p), preprocessing O(p log p)

falsifiers_triggered: Known classical hidden-shifted-power algorithms already use logarithmically many queries with domain-scale time.
Fixed public query prefixes admit polynomial-online decoding after domain-size preprocessing on every tested row.
No unconditional superpolynomial decoding lower bound or natural-problem reduction is present.

preprocessing_records.access_model: fixed chosen queries plus public modulus-dependent nonuniform preprocessing/advice
preprocessing_records.interpretation: A fixed public prefix uniquely fingerprints every shift and a precomputed domain-size table decodes the observed prefix online. Online query/time cost is polynomial in log p, but preprocessing and advice remain Theta(p log p), exponential in the encoded input length.

summary: Audited 12 shifted-character preprocessing row(s) and 48 literature upper-bound row(s). Fixed O(log p)-scale prefixes plus domain-size advice decoded 12 row(s); no unconditional superpolynomial decoding lower bound or natural-problem reduction is recorded.
